title: Thoughts and Things 7.25.2025 — pre-Austin sacrifice journal
html:
TL;DR

  • Late-July 2025 journal six weeks before the Austin move — Yahir is missing income, housing, and "what I'll actually do once I'm there."
  • Compares the situation to his 6 month abroad decision: outline plan, next step determined on the spot. Same dad-conflict pattern repeats — pre-India departure, dad voiced discontent at being left out; same conversation just happened again pre-Austin.
  • "2090 miles away or 31 hours by car" — names the distance from Grants Pass to Austin as a measurable weight.
  • Articulates the family-mission line: "My mom will have to work 7 days a week until she dies. My dad will side gig and finesse until he dies. I am the one that will need to break this."
  • Tension named explicitly: the sacrifice required is also counterproductive to time with parents who want to see him.

Notes

This is a load-bearing family as hidden runway document. The line "I am willing to drown" is the extremism dial turned to max — but immediately followed by the doubt: "the sacrifices may also be counterproductive."

The dad-pattern repetition (didn't share the india plan, dad upset → didn't share the austin move plan, dad upset again) is a family as hidden runway signature: Yahir solo-runs his life decisions, parents feel locked out, conflict reopens each launch. The line "getting insights and help from people that want to help is the meta" is the lesson he's still trying to internalize.

Also notable: this is one of the cleanest articulations of the mom mission — "no one else will and I have always known that I am the one meant to do this." Slots into founder loneliness 2022 2026 as the pre-arrival emotional baseline.

Document cuts off mid-sentence ("the sacrufues i am mentioning are con-").

Connections

  • family as hidden runway · founder loneliness 2022 2026 — the synthesis arcs
  • austin move · shanghai study abroad — the two parallel commit moments
  • mom yahir · dad yahir — the parental tension
  • grants pass or · austin tx — the geographic weight

Privacy

  • No PII, no third-party identifiers beyond family.
